mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% triggers a labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 104 signals a supply chain disruption that reduces corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 104 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary easing.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 16800 signals a strong demand environment that increases corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods with real GDP above 16800 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 16800 and a decline in the housing market index below 1100 signals a strong demand environment and a housing market slowdown that increases corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with both GDPC1 > 16800 and HOUST < 1100 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of labor market slowdown.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial unemployment claims below 220000 signals a labor market improvement that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 220000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism's direction flips in the presence of monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the real sales of domestic product above 470000 signals a supply chain improvement that increases corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in periods with real sales of domestic product above 470000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
